response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (founder of GISCollective, Green Map GIS applications, FOSS4G speaker) and proven low-latency systems work (DLang microservices handling millions of requests/second with async I/O and fibers at Dunnhumby), which maps well to the routing tile pipeline domain. However, his commercial C++ experience is limited and dated (a 7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 plus a thesis project), he has no Rust, and his recent years are dominated by TypeScript/Node full-stack product work rather than systems engineering. There is no direct navigation/routing experience, so he would need to demonstrate transferable systems depth in an interview.
strengths: Real geospatial data platform experience as founder/maintainer of GISCollective and Green Map GIS apps
Low-latency, high-throughput systems background (DLang, async I/O, caching, millions of HTTP req/s)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling author, Linux and Kubernetes operations
concerns: Modern C++ experience is thin and ~10 years old; no Rust listed
No direct routing, navigation guidance, or map-tile pipeline experience
Recent roles (2020-present) are TypeScript/Node product work, drifting away from systems-level engineering
